stages.name: targeted_unit_tests
stages.evidence: ai_commands_test (provider resolution incl. AI-disabled/provider-not-allowed, default report path + report-not-found, durable/display mutant-ref resolution scoped to the report + target-not-found, prompt-envelope validation embedding ai.context.v1 with command failure_kind plus schema-version-only and unknown-context rejection, explain/suggest/review-tests response validation including doctest classifications and malformed-output rejection, and stub-provider text/JSON snapshots) passes; full suite green

stages.name: property_tests
stages.evidence: The response validator rejects every malformed/unsafe shape (non-object, unknown classification/confidence, unknown field including a status-setting field, hidden tool instructions, >3 suggestions, non-project-relative target path, non-durable mutant id); the prompt envelope always embeds a context that passes the registered ai.context.v1 structural validator; AI output never sets a mutant status, report status, or any deterministic result field

stages.name: cli_smoke
stages.evidence: explain/suggest/review-tests render advisory text/JSON and exit 0 with the stub provider; AI-disabled, remote-not-allowed, missing-report, and unknown-target invocations exit 2 with their ZNTL_AI_* codes on stderr and never touch a report

stages.name: mutation_checks
stages.reason: self-mutation dogfood of the AI command engine is gated to the Phase 5 stages (task 085); the advisory contracts are deterministic and covered by unit and property-style tests, and AI is advisory-only so it cannot influence mutant classification or report status

residual_risk: The stub provider is the only provider exercised here; local and remote modes return the same deterministic stub responses, and live remote calls remain out of scope (non-goal) until a future provider task.

review-tests anchors its prompt context on the first survivor while the response clusters all survivors deterministically from the report; a richer multi-survivor prompt context is a later refinement and does not affect the advisory-only guarantee.
